channel.title: Programming on Nerd things and more...
channel.description: Recent content in Programming on Nerd things and more...

item.title: MouseJiggler
item.description:
In KMK there is a module called Mouse Jiggler which allows you to "periodically jiggle the mouse cursor, keeping the host system from idling or sleeping."

It works really fine, since the keyboard sends HID commands to move the mouse and all apps in Windows recognize the movement. But what if I do not have my keyboard around me all the time? can't I just make an script to jiggle the mouse?

item.title: Automating with Python: A start
item.description:
I remember once hearing "No matter what you do for living, learn a programming language it will ease your life."

Although, using Linux takes you to a long trip of practicing scripting with bash, I was never really into programming (still not, eventhough I would have liked at some point).

From my school years, I think the first programming language I was in touch with was C++ in highschool.
